 {"id":"wa-lfts","title":"Set up automated nightly rebase CI for WezTerm fork","description":"## Overview

WezTerm is updated nightly by its author. To maintain our feature branch, we need automated rebasing with conflict detection and alerting.

## Prerequisites

- Completed: wa-ixt4 (WezTerm fork with wa integration)

## CI Workflow Design

### GitHub Actions Workflow

\`\`\`.github/workflows/rebase-upstream.yml\`\`\`
\`\`\`yaml
name: Rebase on Upstream WezTerm

on:
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 6 * * *'  # Daily at 6 AM UTC
  workflow_dispatch:      # Manual trigger

jobs:
  rebase:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
          ref: feature/wa-integration
          token: \${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
      
      - name: Configure Git
        run: |
          git config user.name "wa-bot"
          git config user.email "wa-bot@example.com"
      
      - name: Add Upstream Remote
        run: |
          git remote add upstream https://github.com/wez/wezterm.git
          git fetch upstream main
      
      - name: Attempt Rebase
        id: rebase
        continue-on-error: true
        run: |
          git rebase upstream/main
          echo "rebase_status=\$?" >> \$GITHUB_OUTPUT
      
      - name: Push if Successful
        if: steps.rebase.outcome == 'success'
        run: |
          git push --force-with-lease origin feature/wa-integration
      
      - name: Create Conflict Issue
        if: steps.rebase.outcome == 'failure'
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: |
            const { data: issues } = await github.rest.issues.listForRepo({
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              labels: 'rebase-conflict',
              state: 'open'
            });
            
            if (issues.length === 0) {
              await github.rest.issues.create({
                owner: context.repo.owner,
                repo: context.repo.repo,
                title: 'Rebase conflict with upstream WezTerm',
                body: \`The nightly rebase on upstream WezTerm failed due to conflicts.
                
                **Action Required**: Manual resolution needed.
                
                \\\`\\\`\\\`bash
                git fetch upstream main
                git rebase upstream/main
                # Resolve conflicts
                git rebase --continue
                git push --force-with-lease
                \\\`\\\`\\\`
                \`,
                labels: ['rebase-conflict', 'urgent']
              });
            }
      
      - name: Run Tests After Rebase
        if: steps.rebase.outcome == 'success'
        run: |
          cargo build --features wa-integration
          cargo test --features wa-integration

  notify:
    needs: rebase
    if: failure()
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Send Notification
        run: |
          # Notify via email, Slack, Discord, etc.
          echo "Rebase failed - notification would be sent"
\`\`\`

### Handling Conflicts

When conflicts occur:
1. CI creates GitHub issue with "rebase-conflict" label
2. Human/agent reviews conflicting files
3. Usually conflicts are in files we modified (minimal set)
4. Resolve, test, push

### Minimizing Conflicts

To reduce conflict frequency:
1. Keep wa changes behind \`#[cfg(feature)]\` — no logic changes to upstream code
2. Use separate files where possible (mux/src/wa_events.rs)
3. Add wa code at end of functions, not middle
4. Avoid reformatting upstream code

## Alternative: Patch-Based Approach

Instead of maintaining a branch, maintain a patch series:

\`\`\`bash
# Generate patches
git format-patch upstream/main..feature/wa-integration -o patches/

# Apply patches to fresh clone
git clone --depth=1 https://github.com/wez/wezterm.git
cd wezterm
git am ../patches/*.patch
\`\`\`

Patches are more explicit about what we change, easier to review.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] GitHub Actions workflow created
- [ ] Nightly rebase runs automatically
- [ ] Conflicts create GitHub issues
- [ ] Notifications configured
- [ ] Post-rebase tests run
- [ ] Documentation for manual conflict resolution
- [ ] 30-day trial period: track conflict frequency

## Files to Create

- .github/workflows/rebase-upstream.yml
- docs/maintaining-wezterm-fork.md

## References

- GitHub Actions: https://docs.github.com/en/actions
- Git rebase: https://git-scm.com/docs/git-rebase
- WezTerm release cadence: nightly builds","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:49:53.398103664Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T05:47:48.251435Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T06:52:05.800499Z","close_reason":"Superseded by wa-2umk2: Nightly rebase CI no longer needed — we own the source","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-lfts","depends_on_id":"wa-bjm9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:21Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-lfts","depends_on_id":"wa-ixt4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:21Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"wa-lh17","title":"FTUI-02.2 Define upstream sync/pinning policy for /dp/frankentui","description":"## Background\nUsing ftui across the board requires predictable intake of upstream changes.\n\n## Deliverables\n- version pin + bump cadence\n- change-review checklist for upstream updates\n- compatibility risk checklist for API drift\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- policy is documented and automated where feasible\n- upgrade path is reproducible by future maintainers.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:07:41.205715220Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-11T05:47:48.220224Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T01:32:32.242240956Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-lh17","depends_on_id":"wa-ju1x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"wa-lkaa","title":"Implement Slack/Discord/email/webhook senders","description":"## What
Implement channel-specific senders using the unified notification interface.

## Why
Operators rely on existing collaboration tools; we should reuse existing webhook templates and add email without duplicating logic.

## How
- Slack/Discord: reuse existing webhook template renderers
- Generic webhook: existing path + new interface
- Email: add SMTP sender with redacted summaries

## Success Criteria
- Each sender validates config and handles failures gracefully
- Payloads include redacted summaries and actionable links","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilentCanyon","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:08:08.242237143Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T05:47:48.264364Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T16:09:51.381960390Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-lkaa","depends_on_id":"wa-itft","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-lkaa","depends_on_id":"wa-j1ke","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"wa-lr93","title":"Automatic Restart Scheduling","description":"## Goal
Automatically schedule optimal mux server restart times by finding windows where the hazard rate is rising but agent activity is at a local minimum — "restart when it's safest and most needed."

## Background & Motivation
wa-1igc provides safe restart capability. wa-1qz1.1 predicts failure probability. But nothing decides WHEN to restart. The user currently restarts manually when things get bad, losing work in the process.

Automatic scheduling means: "FrankenTerm will restart itself at 3:17am tonight because the hazard rate is projected to cross 0.8 at 5am, and agent activity drops to a daily minimum between 2-4am."

## Technical Design

### Optimal Window Algorithm
Score each future time window (t, t+Δ) by:
  score(t) = hazard_urgency(t) × activity_minimum(t) × recency_penalty(t)

Where:
- hazard_urgency(t) = sigmoid(H(t) - H_threshold) — how urgently restart is needed
- activity_minimum(t) = 1 - normalized_activity(t) — prefer low-activity periods
- recency_penalty(t) = 1 - exp(-t_since_last_restart / cooldown) — avoid too-frequent restarts

### Activity Prediction
Use 24-hour periodicity in agent activity:
- Maintain hourly activity profile (EWMA of output rates per hour-of-day)
- Predict future activity: activity(t) ≈ profile[hour_of_day(t)]
- Low-activity windows are ideal restart times

### Scheduling Modes
```rust
pub enum RestartMode {
    /// Fully automatic — restart when score exceeds threshold
    Automatic { min_score: f64 },
    /// Suggest only — alert user with recommended window
    Advisory,
    /// Disabled — manual only
    Manual,
}
```

### Pre-Restart Workflow
When restart is scheduled:
1. T-30min: Alert user (optional, configurable)
2. T-5min: Trigger full snapshot
3. T-0: Execute safe restart (wa-1igc)
4. T+1min: Verify restoration

### Implementation Location
- New file: crates/wa-core/src/restart_scheduler.rs
- Integration: survival model + activity profiling + wa-1igc safe restart

## Configuration
```toml
[restart_scheduler]
mode = "advisory"             # "automatic" | "advisory" | "manual"
min_score_threshold = 0.7     # Minimum score for automatic restart
cooldown_hours = 12           # Minimum time between restarts
advance_warning_minutes = 30  # Alert before restart
pre_restart_snapshot = true   # Full snapshot before restart
```

## Dependencies
- wa-1qz1.1 (survival model): provides hazard rate projections
- wa-1igc (safe restart): executes the actual restart
- wa-29k1 (SnapshotEngine): pre-restart snapshot

## Acceptance Criteria
- Score-based window selection combining hazard and activity
- 24-hour activity profile learning
- Advisory mode alerts with recommended window
- Automatic mode with configurable safety thresholds
- Pre-restart snapshot guarantee
- Unit tests: synthetic hazard + activity curves, verify optimal window selection

## Benchmark Requirements
- **criterion benchmarks** with target `benches/restart_scheduling.rs`:
  - `scheduling_decision_latency`: measure time to evaluate all candidate windows and select the optimal restart time given a 24-hour activity profile and hazard curve. Target: <1ms for scoring 1,440 one-minute windows (24 hours).
  - `activity_profile_update`: measure EWMA update latency when incorporating new hourly activity data. Target: <10us per update.
  - `hazard_urgency_computation`: measure sigmoid-based urgency scoring for a batch of 100 candidate times. Target: <50us total.

## Property-Based Testing (proptest)
- **Schedule optimality under constraints**: for any valid hazard curve and activity profile, the selected restart window has a score >= all other candidate windows that satisfy the cooldown constraint. No valid window with a higher score is skipped.
- **Cooldown enforcement**: for any sequence of scheduled restarts, consecutive restarts are always separated by at least `cooldown_hours`. No scheduling decision violates the cooldown.
- **Monotonic urgency**: if `hazard_rate(t1) > hazard_rate(t2)` and `activity(t1) == activity(t2)`, then `score(t1) >= score(t2)`. Higher hazard always increases urgency when activity is equal.
- **Advisory consistency**: in Advisory mode, the recommended window is identical to what Automatic mode would select (same scoring, just no execution).

## Cross-References
- **wa-2imn** (Intelligent snapshot scheduling): coordinates with restart scheduling to ensure snapshots are taken at complementary times; restart scheduling should trigger a snapshot before restart, while wa-2imn handles routine periodic snapshots during normal operation.
- **wa-1igc** (Safe-restart workflow): the execution engine that this scheduler invokes; restart scheduling decides WHEN, wa-1igc decides HOW (atomic snapshot-restart-restore cycle).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-09T22:43:42.695393Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-10T19:46:50.587212Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-lr93","depends_on_id":"wa-1igc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T23:50:42.122524Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-lr93","depends_on_id":"wa-1qz1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T22:45:43.010096Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-lr93","depends_on_id":"wa-29k1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T22:45:46.436752Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-lr93","depends_on_id":"wa-rsaf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T22:45:24.304114Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"wa-ls0w","title":"Remove STATUS_UPDATE_LUA from setup.rs (with deprecation path)","description":"## Overview

Remove the STATUS_UPDATE_LUA constant and its injection into wezterm.lua from crates/wa-core/src/setup.rs. This is the core change that eliminates the Lua performance bottleneck.

## Current State

In setup.rs (lines 54-105), STATUS_UPDATE_LUA is defined:
\`\`\`rust
const STATUS_UPDATE_LUA: &str = r#"-- Forward pane status updates to wa daemon (rate-limited)
local wa_last_status_update = {}
local WA_STATUS_UPDATE_INTERVAL_MS = 2000

wezterm.on('update-status', function(window, pane)
  // ... 50+ lines of Lua code
end)"#;
\`\`\`

This is injected via:
- \`create_wa_block()\` (line 837-838) — combines USERVAR_FORWARDING_LUA + STATUS_UPDATE_LUA
- \`generate_ssh_domains_lua()\` (lines 708-711) — appends both snippets

## Changes Required

### 1. Remove STATUS_UPDATE_LUA Constant
Delete the entire constant definition (lines 49-105).

### 2. Update create_wa_block()
Before:
\`\`\`rust
fn create_wa_block() -> String {
    format!("{WA_BEGIN_MARKER}\\n{USERVAR_FORWARDING_LUA}\\n\\n{STATUS_UPDATE_LUA}\\n{WA_END_MARKER}")
}
\`\`\`

After:
\`\`\`rust
fn create_wa_block() -> String {
    format!("{WA_BEGIN_MARKER}\\n{USERVAR_FORWARDING_LUA}\\n{WA_END_MARKER}")
}
\`\`\`

### 3. Update generate_ssh_domains_lua()
Remove STATUS_UPDATE_LUA from the generated block:
\`\`\`rust
// Line 708-711, remove:
output.push_str(STATUS_UPDATE_LUA);
output.push('\\n');
\`\`\`

### 4. Update Test Assertions
Several tests check for STATUS_UPDATE_LUA content:
- test_create_wa_block_format() — update assertions
- test_generate_ssh_domains_block_includes_hosts_and_snippets() — remove STATUS_UPDATE_LUA assertion

### 5. Migration Path for Existing Users

Users who already ran \`wa setup\` have STATUS_UPDATE_LUA in their wezterm.lua. Options:

**Option A: Silent removal on next setup**
- \`wa setup\` replaces the entire WA-managed block
- Old STATUS_UPDATE_LUA code gets removed automatically
- Simplest, but users don't know what changed

**Option B: Explicit migration command (Recommended)**
- Add \`wa setup --upgrade\` or \`wa setup migrate\`
- Shows diff of what will change
- Requires user confirmation
- Document in release notes

**Option C: Deprecation warning**
- Keep STATUS_UPDATE_LUA for one version cycle
- Print deprecation warning on \`wa watch\` startup if status updates detected
- Remove in next major version

**Recommendation**: Implement Option B with clear messaging.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] STATUS_UPDATE_LUA constant removed from setup.rs
- [ ] create_wa_block() only includes USERVAR_FORWARDING_LUA
- [ ] generate_ssh_domains_lua() only includes USERVAR_FORWARDING_LUA
- [ ] All tests updated and passing
- [ ] \`wa setup\` cleanly upgrades existing configs
- [ ] Documentation updated (CHANGELOG, migration notes)

## Backward Compatibility

- Existing wezterm.lua files with STATUS_UPDATE_LUA will continue to work
- They'll just be wasteful (sending events wa ignores)
- Running \`wa setup\` again will remove the old code

## Files to Modify

- crates/wa-core/src/setup.rs:
  - Remove STATUS_UPDATE_LUA constant (lines 49-105)
  - Update create_wa_block() (line 837)
  - Update generate_ssh_domains_lua() (lines 708-711)
- crates/wa-core/src/setup.rs tests:
  - test_create_wa_block_format()
  - test_generate_ssh_domains_block_includes_hosts_and_snippets()

## Dependencies

- Depends on: wa-5jim (ingest loop provides all metadata)
- Depends on: wa-mw44 (alt-screen detection works)

## References

- setup.rs: crates/wa-core/src/setup.rs
- WA-managed block format: lines 20-31 (markers)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:45:04.261485776Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T05:47:48.315774Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T22:23:47.733158771Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-ls0w","depends_on_id":"wa-5jim","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-ls0w","depends_on_id":"wa-8wrn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-ls0w","depends_on_id":"wa-mw44","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"wa-lusg","title":"Update E2E tests: remove status_update scenario, add alt-screen detection test","description":"## Overview

Update the E2E test suite to reflect the removal of STATUS_UPDATE_LUA and addition of alt-screen detection via escape sequences.

## Current E2E Test State

The E2E test harness (scripts/e2e_test.sh) has a "status_update" scenario that:
1. Writes a minimal wezterm.lua with STATUS_UPDATE_LUA
2. Spawns a pane
3. Sends a status update via \`wa event --from-status\`
4. Verifies the watcher receives it

This scenario will become invalid and must be removed.

## Changes Required

### 1. Remove status_update Scenario

In SCENARIO_REGISTRY (~line 340):
\`\`\`bash
# REMOVE this entry:
"status_update:Validate status update forwarding lane (wezterm.lua -> wa event -> watcher)"
\`\`\`

Remove the entire \`run_scenario_status_update()\` function (~lines 2550-2650).

### 2. Add alt-screen Detection Scenario

New scenario: "alt_screen_detection"

Purpose: Verify wa detects alt-screen transitions via escape sequence parsing (not Lua).

Test flow:
\`\`\`bash
run_scenario_alt_screen_detection() {
    # 1. Start wa watch
    # 2. Spawn a pane
    # 3. Send ESC[?1049h (enter alt-screen) to pane
    # 4. Verify wa detects alt-screen = true
    # 5. Send ESC[?1049l (leave alt-screen)
    # 6. Verify wa detects alt-screen = false
}
\`\`\`

Implementation sketch:
\`\`\`bash
run_scenario_alt_screen_detection() {
    local scenario_dir="$1"
    local wait_timeout="${TEST_WAIT_TIMEOUT:-30}"
    
    log_step "Starting wa watch..."
    wa watch --foreground > "$scenario_dir/wa_watch.log" 2>&1 &
    local wa_pid=$!
    sleep 2
    
    log_step "Spawning test pane..."
    local pane_id=$(wezterm cli spawn --cwd /tmp -- bash)
    sleep 1
    
    log_step "Verifying initial state (not alt-screen)..."
    local state=$(wa robot state --format json)
    local is_alt=$(echo "$state" | jq -r ".data.panes[] | select(.pane_id == $pane_id) | .is_alt_screen")
    if [[ "$is_alt" != "false" ]]; then
        log_fail "Expected is_alt_screen=false initially, got: $is_alt"
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_step "Sending alt-screen enter sequence..."
    # ESC [ ? 1049 h
    printf '\e[?1049h' | wezterm cli send-text --pane-id "$pane_id" --no-paste
    sleep 0.5  # Allow detection
    
    log_step "Verifying alt-screen detected..."
    state=$(wa robot state --format json)
    is_alt=$(echo "$state" | jq -r ".data.panes[] | select(.pane_id == $pane_id) | .is_alt_screen")
    if [[ "$is_alt" != "true" ]]; then
        log_fail "Expected is_alt_screen=true after ESC[?1049h, got: $is_alt"
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_step "Sending alt-screen leave sequence..."
    # ESC [ ? 1049 l
    printf '\e[?1049l' | wezterm cli send-text --pane-id "$pane_id" --no-paste
    sleep 0.5
    
    log_step "Verifying alt-screen exited..."
    state=$(wa robot state --format json)
    is_alt=$(echo "$state" | jq -r ".data.panes[] | select(.pane_id == $pane_id) | .is_alt_screen")
    if [[ "$is_alt" != "false" ]]; then
        log_fail "Expected is_alt_screen=false after ESC[?1049l, got: $is_alt"
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_pass "Alt-screen detection via escape sequences works!"
    cleanup_pane "$pane_id"
    kill "$wa_pid" 2>/dev/null
}
\`\`\`

Verify it doesn't depend on status update functionality.
